# Vendors / Partners — Parked for Future Evaluation

**Purpose:** Capture vendor / partner pitches that aren't right for
the current moment but are worth revisiting later. Each entry has
enough info to act on without keeping the physical paper.

**Review cadence:** Quarterly, or when a relevant workstream opens.

---

## Nimbus Healthcare (captured 5/29/26)

**Pitch:** *"Your patients, your revenue"* — B2B program for
chiropractors to offer **GLP-1 weight-loss management, peptides,
hormones, inflammation protocols** as a revenue stream in their
practice. Typically structured with Nimbus providing the
MD-of-record so DCs can offer the program even though they can't
prescribe directly.

**Contact:**
- Email: `providers@nimbushealthcare.com`
- Website: `provider.nimbushealthcare.com`
- QR code on the original card (not transcribed — use the website)

**Why parked, not pursued now:**

- Robert's 2026 focus is Channel A (CA EDD SDI) + Channel B (Veteran
  SCD) + CSA Vol 1/Vol 2 architecture. Adding a separate
  revenue-stream evaluation right now adds context-switch cost.
- License + tax stabilization is the foundation work through Q3.
- GLP-1s are MD-prescribed; integration would require accepting the
  Nimbus partnership-with-MD-of-record model. Worth thinking
  through brand fit before signing.

**Why it's worth keeping:**

- Sleep apnea correlates strongly with obesity. GLP-1 weight loss
  is a clinically-established adjunct to OSA / UAOSA care.
- Fits naturally into **Chiro 3.0** (Telehealth / EDD / Disability
  / Recovery track) as an add-on revenue line.
- SDI patients on recovery arcs may benefit from weight management
  as part of returning to work readiness.
- The "chiropractor + GLP-1 partnership" niche is real and growing
  in 2026.

**When to revisit:**

- Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 — after Wichita stabilization, when the SDI
  patient touchpoint sequence is running and there's bandwidth to
  evaluate add-on revenue lines.

**Open questions for that evaluation:**

- What's Nimbus's partnership economics (% of revenue? per-patient
  fee? flat platform fee)?
- Does the Nimbus MD operate as the patient's prescribing physician
  in CA telehealth law?
- Is GLP-1 management consistent with CSA's structural-airway brand,
  or does it dilute focus? (Honest read: it's a Chiro 3.0 add-on,
  not a Chiro 2.0 element — keep separate brand lanes.)
- What's the patient acquisition cost vs. lifetime value for a
  GLP-1 program?

## Peptides 180 / Pure Fusion (captured 5/29/26)

**Pitch:** *"Scientific performance through innovation — built for
human optimization."* B2B partner program for chiropractors,
medical professionals, and wellness clinics to sell compounded
peptides (in-clinic or drop-shipped to patients).

**Contact:**
- POC: Jeff Fuller, Clinical Liaison & Peptide Specialist
- Email: `jj@peptides180.com`
- Phone: 469-847-2148
- Website: `www.peptides180.com`

**Product categories:**
- Cognitive Mood
- Hormone Growth Support
- **Sleep** ← potentially CSA-adjacent
- Libido
- Aesthetics and Longevity
- Weight Management and Metabolism
- Performance and Recovery

**Manufacturing:** FDA-registered pharmacies in AZ / NV / CA / WA;
GMP standards; COAs (Certificates of Analysis) available on request.

**Partnership terms (per the brochure):**
- Wholesale pricing + practitioner support
- Educational resources + stack protocols
- Marketing tools + white-label opportunities
- In-clinic stocking OR drop-ship to patient
- "Fusion Network" branded partnership

**Brand-voice flag (5/29/26):** Robert specifically noted aversion
to the *"pharmacy-grade"* and *"pharmaceutical-grade"* language used
in this brochure. See CLAUDE.md §4 brand voice updates. If CSA ever
partners with Peptides 180, the messaging would need rebranding away
from pharma-grade framing toward the CSA voice (structural,
mechanism-based, anatomical).

**Why parked:**
- Same logic as Nimbus — Chiro 3.0 fit but not 2026 focus
- License + tax stabilization is the foundation work
- Brand-voice mismatch needs addressing before partnership

**Why kept:**
- The **Sleep category** specifically overlaps with CSA's core lane
- Could support the 12-Module Stay-at-Home Program (§17) as a
  sleep-adjacent revenue line
- The Fusion Network white-label model would let CSA offer peptides
  under its own brand if structured well

**Revisit:** Q4 2026 / Q1 2027 post-Wichita.

## PACE (Federation of Chiropractic Licensing Boards) — Kansas reference

Not a vendor relationship; captured for future operational reference
when Robert relocates to Wichita 8/14/26.

**What PACE is:**
- The Federation of Chiropractic Licensing Boards' CE-credit
  recognition program
- Courses that meet PACE criteria are auto-approved for CE credit
  in PACE-recognized state boards (saves applying for each course
  individually)

**Key fact:**
- **California does NOT accept PACE** (so currently irrelevant for
  Robert's CA license)
- **Kansas DOES accept PACE** — when Robert relocates to Wichita and
  potentially adds a KS license OR maintains CE compliance in
  multiple states, PACE becomes useful

**Contact:**
- Website: `PACEX.fclb.org`
- Phone: 970-356-3500
- Email: `pace@fclb.org`
- Address: 5401 W 10th St, Suite 101, Greeley, CO 80634

**Revisit:** August 2026 as part of the Wichita relocation prep,
when KS-license decisions come into focus.

## Sarah Atwater — Trillium Law Course (captured 5/29/26) — ⚠️ CAUTION

**Pitch:** Create your own private church, ministry, and trust.
*"Spiritually grounded lawful framework for stewardship, autonomy,
and private operation."*

**Self-described:** "New earth systems consultant" who created her
own private church + ministry in 2022, formed a ministry trust + a
foreign trust integrated into those structures.

**Contact:**
- Phone (WhatsApp): 506-646-9531 (New Brunswick, Canada area code)
- Email/website: `Sarah@water.mykajabi.com` *(Kajabi is a course
  platform; URL syntax suggests her course is hosted on Kajabi —
  verify exact URL)*

**Course offerings (Trillium Law Course):**

| Package | Price | What's included |
|---|---|---|
| **Silver** | $3,500 | Full church/ministry/trust course + document review + private consultations |
| **Gold** | $5,500 | Advanced support for complex projects, document review, 12 hours of individualized support |
| **Private consult** | $250/hr | A la carte |

**What the course teaches:**
- Private membership association
- Church and ministry creation
- Private non-taxable trust for holding/protecting assets
- Supporting documents for "lawful private operation"

**Pitched benefits:**
- Operate within private faith-based framework rather than public
  commercial systems
- Foundation for private trust + private trust bank account
- Protect spiritually grounded work through "conscience, consent,
  and fiduciary duty"
- Accept non-taxable + tax-deductible donations
- Private dispute resolution mechanisms

### ⚠️ WHY THIS NEEDS SERIOUS CAUTION

**The IRS aggressively challenges "private trust" / "ministerial
trust" / "religious tax shelter" structures.** These have a long,
well-documented enforcement history. Key concerns:

1. **IRS view of abusive trust schemes:** The IRS publishes a regular
   "Dirty Dozen" tax scams list. Private trust / mail-order ministry
   / "sovereign citizen" trust structures appear on it frequently.
   Marketing claims like "non-taxable" + "lawful private operation"
   + "outside public commercial systems" are textbook IRS red flags.
2. **Robert is currently facing ~$434K IRS liability** across 4 tax
   years (see `legal/active-paperwork.md` Subcategory 4). Creating
   a private trust NOW, before the IRS matter is resolved, would
   likely:
   - Be characterized as fraudulent conveyance (attempting to shield
     assets from IRS collection)
   - Subject the trust to "alter ego" / "nominee" challenges
   - Potentially increase Robert's legal exposure rather than
     reduce it
3. **"Religious" / "ministerial" tax exemptions** require actual
   501(c)(3) status from the IRS — which has rigorous documentation,
   ongoing reporting, and operational requirements. A "private
   church" without IRS recognition has no tax benefits and creates
   filing complications.
4. **Legitimate asset protection** exists (LLCs, S-Corps, IRA
   structures, retirement-plan protections, homestead exemptions,
   spendthrift trusts) — but they're built by **tax attorneys and
   estate-planning attorneys**, not by "new earth systems consultants"
   selling $3,500-$5,500 courses.
5. **Robert's broader worldview** (Anarchapulco / Liberpulco /
   sovereignty / Tom Cowan crowd) overlaps significantly with the
   audience that these courses target. The pitch is designed to
   appeal to that worldview. **That doesn't make it correct or
   safe.** Robert's discernment moment ("being recruited into a
   movement" — CLAUDE.md §19) is the right instinct to apply here.

### What Robert should NOT do

- ❌ Pay for Silver or Gold package before getting independent legal
  advice from a tax attorney
- ❌ Form any "private trust" structure while the IRS matter is
  active
- ❌ Combine this with any anti-IRS / sovereign-citizen tax theories
  that may circulate in this community
- ❌ Use this course as a substitute for legitimate asset protection
  + estate planning

### What Robert COULD do

- ✅ Ask the tax attorney handling the IRS matter about:
  - Legitimate asset protection options for his situation
  - Estate planning given his children + Dusanka claim + future
    Wichita move
  - Whether any "private trust" structure has *any* legitimate
    application for his case
- ✅ Read Sarah's content for the sovereignty / spiritual framework
  it represents WITHOUT acting on the legal structures it pitches

### Decision

**PARK — do NOT engage until tax attorney is consulted.** If the
attorney sees any legitimate use case for trust structures in
Robert's situation, the attorney can recommend a licensed CA tax/
estate-planning specialist. Sarah's course is not a substitute for
licensed legal counsel.

**Revisit:** After tax attorney engagement and IRS matter
stabilization — likely Q1 2027 or later.
